page: Amazon Web Services c8in.large is a compute-optimized server belonging to the c8in family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This server operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance type.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8in.large offers a dedicated compute profile with a low resource footprint, making it suitable for entry-level compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory allocations or local NVMe storage. Given its compute-optimized design, 3.9 GHz Intel processor, and 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is suited for lightweight web serving, network-optimized microservices, batch processing, and CI/CD runner tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8in.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel processor. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from a single physical core with 2 threads, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory. This instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached EBS volumes, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Designed for compute-bound workloads with a low memory footprint, the c8in.large offers a cost-effective entry point for dedicated CPU resources. It is suited for lightweight web serving, network-optimized microservices, batch processing, and CI/CD pipeline tasks.
